title: garlicky chewy syrian mung-bean-sprout-sukju pastry-flattened buns
description: festival food featuring mung bean sprouts, suku seeds, and garlicky pastry dough. great as a side dish or snack.
instructions: in a small bowl, mix together warm milk, sugar, and yeast. let it sit for 10 minutes until foamy.
add vegetable oil, salt, and 1 cup of flour to the bowl. mix until a sticky dough forms.
add mung bean sprouts, suku seeds, and minced garlic to the dough. mix until well combined.
add the remaining 1/2 cup of flour and mix until the dough becomes smooth and elastic.
knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes chewy and sticky.
cover the dough with plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
preheat oil in a deep frying pan over medium-high heat.
divide the dough into 8 equal pieces. roll each piece into a ball and flatten it slightly.
fry the buns in hot oil for 2-3 minutes on each side until golden brown and crispy.
drain excess oil on paper towels and serve hot.
